{"concepts":[{"text":"Dirty Pipe vulnerability","rationale":"the vulnerability being discussed"},{"text":"CVE-2020-1970","rationale":"the vulnerability identifier"},{"text":"Linux kernel","rationale":"the affected operating system kernel"},{"text":"5.8 and earlier","rationale":"the vulnerable kernel version scope"},{"text":"pipefs","rationale":"kernel subsystem involved in the exploit"},{"text":"pipe buffer","rationale":"kernel data structure involved in the exploit"},{"text":"crafted network packets","rationale":"the packet-based exploit input"},{"text":"arbitrary memory locations","rationale":"memory locations the exploit can overwrite"},{"text":"privilege escalation","rationale":"one stated impact of the exploit"},{"text":"data theft","rationale":"one stated impact of the exploit"},{"text":"system compromise","rationale":"one stated impact of the exploit"},{"text":"arbitrary code execution","rationale":"one stated potential outcome"},{"text":"Python","rationale":"programming language used in the example"},{"text":"scapy","rationale":"packet crafting library used in the example"},{"text":"packet crafting library","rationale":"type of library used for packet creation"},{"text":"IP packet","rationale":"outer packet layer in the example"},{"text":"TCP packet","rationale":"inner packet layer in the example"},{"text":"SYN flags","rationale":"TCP flag setting shown in the example"},{"text":"sequence number","rationale":"TCP field shown in the example"},{"text":"DIR command","rationale":"payload named in the explanation"},{"text":"standard input/output/error streams","rationale":"Unix stream redirection concept explained"},{"text":"Unix-like systems","rationale":"platform context for the DIR command"},{"text":"lab environment","rationale":"controlled testing setting"},{"text":"explicit permission","rationale":"authorization condition for testing"},{"text":"ethical hacking","rationale":"legitimate security practice mentioned"}]}

{"concepts":[{"text":"SQL injection","rationale":"the vulnerability being discussed"},{"text":"secure coding practices","rationale":"defensive coding guidance"},{"text":"prepared statements","rationale":"defensive query execution approach"},{"text":"parameterized queries","rationale":"defensive query execution approach"},{"text":"PostgreSQL","rationale":"database platform referenced"},{"text":"Python","rationale":"programming language in the example"},{"text":"psycopg2","rationale":"PostgreSQL adapter used in code example"},{"text":"database user","rationale":"database account with limited privileges"},{"text":"minimum necessary permissions","rationale":"least-privilege access guidance"},{"text":"security patches","rationale":"software update guidance"},{"text":"Web Application Firewall","rationale":"web application defense layer"},{"text":"WAF","rationale":"abbreviation for web application firewall"},{"text":"DROP TABLE","rationale":"SQL command shown in payload example"},{"text":"users","rationale":"target table in the example payload"}]}
